rationale: The cited paper appears directly relevant to the citing paper’s core mechanism: both center on two-stage choice processes with heterogeneous/stage-varying decision rules (citing note: two-stage category screening strategies; cited note: empirical two-stage model with varying decision rules and stage-specific criteria). The citing paper also frames itself as refining prior decision-heuristic/multistage choice work, making this citation plausibly methodological or theoretical rather than incidental. Given this strong construct overlap, it should be retained for full relation judgment.
